title: NFL executives reportedly ‘expect’ Patriots to acquire A.J. Brown
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: NFL executives expect the Patriots to acquire Eagles wide receiver A.J. Brown after June 1.
A.J. Brown's potential trade to the Patriots depends on favorable salary-cap implications for the Eagles.
The Eagles reportedly seek a first-round pick and more in exchange for A.J. Brown.

title: 5 must-watch movies & TV shows streaming right now
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: Amanda Seyfried stars in 'The Testament of Ann Lee,' streaming now on Hulu.
'28 Years Later: The Bone Temple' is available for streaming on Netflix.
'Ken Burns Presents: Henry David Thoreau' is streaming on PBS, featuring voices like Jeff Goldblum and George Clooney.

title: Caleb Durbin’s redemption, Willson Contreras’s swagger & other takeaways from Red Sox’ home-opening win
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Red Sox defeated Padres 5-2 in the home opener at Fenway Park, snapping a five-game losing streak.
Caleb Durbin broke a hitless streak with an RBI single, having struggled since joining from the Brewers.
Willson Contreras hit a decisive home run, and Xander Bogaerts got an ovation in his Fenway return despite going hitless.

title: Here are the dates for the Patriots’ offseason workout schedule
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: The Patriots' voluntary offseason program begins April 20 with conditioning drills, meetings, and rehab work.
OTAs for the Patriots will occur on May 27, May 29, June 1-2, June 4, June 8-9, and June 11.
The mandatory minicamp runs from June 15-17, concluding the offseason program before training camp in July.

title: Patriots double up on edge rushers in latest ESPN mock draft
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Patriots select Texas A&M's Cashius Howell and Illinois's Gabe Jacas in ESPN's latest mock draft.
Howell noted for his explosive pressure generating ability; Jacas valued for his NFL-ready run defense.
Patriots also exploring needs in offensive tackle and wide receiver positions amid draft considerations.

title: For a struggling Red Sox team, Friday’s opener at Fenway Park provided a fresh start
author: Trevor Hass
bullets: Red Sox won 5-2 against the Padres in their Fenway Park home opener, improving their record to 2-5.
Sonny Gray pitched six strong innings while Willson Contreras hit the winning home run in the sixth inning.
Caleb Durbin broke a hitting slump with an RBI single, contributing to the Red Sox's first home victory.

title: North Andover police working to get body cameras after Kelsey Fitzsimmons shooting
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: North Andover officials are working to implement a police body-worn camera program nine months after an officer was shot.
Officer Kelsey Fitzsimmons was acquitted after being charged with assault following a confrontation at her home.
Massachusetts has distributed nearly $13 million in grants to local police for body cameras since 2021.

title: Xander Bogaerts talks Roman Anthony, pressure from fans in his return to Fenway Park
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Xander Bogaerts reflects on changes at Fenway Park during his return as a San Diego Padres player.
Bogaerts, 33, signed an 11-year, $280 million contract with the Padres to help them win their first title.
Bogaerts discusses the pressure on Red Sox prospect Roman Anthony, who signed an eight-year, $130 million contract.

title: Step into Boston’s past this season
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: The Freedom Trail in Boston spans 2.5 miles and includes 16 historical sites, attracting over 4 million annual visitors.
Six Flags New England will open the Quantum Accelerator, a dual-launch straddle coaster, starting April 17 for all visitors.
New England residents can access a 10% discount at Top of the Ridge Farm, a bed & breakfast in New Durham, N.H.

title: Singer Vanessa Carlton’s noise complaint against R.I. neighbor ends up before state Supreme Court
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Vanessa Carlton and John McCauley appealed to the Rhode Island Supreme Court over noise issues from neighbors' industrial property.
The couple claims the noise from North American Crane & Rigging LLC exceeds Warwick's 60-decibel limit and disrupts their home life.
Neighbors Artak Avagyan and Lee Beausoleil deny wrongdoing, arguing the injunction is vague and challenging zoning board findings.
